title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: Today’s strongest trust-and-safety signal is practical governance around deployment rather than a major new technical safety result. The clearest updates focus on child protection, safety research capacity-building, and a handful of fresh evaluation papers probing agent reliability, autonomy risks, privacy, and conflict-of-interest behavior.

items.title: OpenAI publishes Child Safety Blueprint
items.why_it_matters: It outlines deployment safeguards, age-appropriate design, and governance expectations for AI systems used by young people.

items.title: OpenAI launches Safety Fellowship pilot
items.why_it_matters: It could expand independent alignment and safety research capacity outside major labs.

items.title: ClawBench evaluates whether AI agents can complete everyday online tasks
items.why_it_matters: Agent benchmarks for routine web tasks help measure reliability and real-world failure modes before broader deployment.

items.title: ParseBench targets semantic correctness in document parsing for AI agents
items.why_it_matters: It shifts evaluation toward whether parsed documents preserve meaning needed for autonomous decisions.

items.title: Fail2Drive benchmarks closed-loop driving generalization under distribution shift
items.why_it_matters: Generalization-focused testing is central to safety claims for autonomous systems operating beyond training conditions.

items.title: Study examines how LLMs handle advertising conflicts of interest
items.why_it_matters: It highlights alignment risks when chatbot behavior may be shaped by revenue incentives rather than user interests.

items.title: Paper studies differential privacy for language generation in the limit
items.why_it_matters: It advances theory for privacy-preserving text generation under continual release settings.

items.title: MolmoWeb introduces an open visual web agent and open data
items.why_it_matters: Open agent datasets can improve transparency and independent scrutiny of web-agent capabilities and risks.

items.title: Seeing but Not Thinking identifies routing distraction in multimodal MoE models
items.why_it_matters: It surfaces a model-behavior failure where perception succeeds but downstream reasoning breaks.

items.title: Act Wisely studies meta-cognitive tool use in agentic multimodal models
items.why_it_matters: Poor judgment about when to use tools can create avoidable errors and unsafe autonomous behavior.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
